results.question: Why was dependency injection introduced in FastAPI?
results.answer: Dependency injection was introduced in FastAPI to allow easy code reuse, share database sessions, enforce security rules, and simplify unit testing by mocking dependencies [PR 42] [Issue 101].
results.ground_truth: Dependency injection was introduced to allow reusable components, share database connections, enforce security/authentication, and simplify testing by mocking dependencies easily.
contexts:

PR Number: #42
Title: Support dependency injection injection
Description: Closes #101. Enables reusable component functions...

Title: Dependency injection support
Body: We need a clean way to share database connections...

results.question: Why did FastAPI migrate to support Pydantic v2?
results.answer: FastAPI migrated to Pydantic v2 to leverage performance improvements (up to 20x faster compilation in Rust), clean validation errors, and updated model configurations [PR 500].
results.ground_truth: FastAPI updated to Pydantic v2 to leverage substantial performance improvements, native Rust validation, cleaner schemas, and updated model configuration structures.
contexts:

PR Number: #500
Title: Upgrade to Pydantic v2
Description: Migrates validation engine to Pydantic v2 for native speedups...
